summary: Mass repository cleanup removing the asm/ folder. The PR API reports 367 changed files, 0 additions, and 1,018,461 deletions; the GitHub diff was too large to retrieve in the slice. The decomp.dev bot reported "No changes" for GALE01 between 39e1688 and d18eb04, indicating the cleanup did not affect the generated/built game output.
what_changed: Removed the asm/ folder according to the PR title.
Deleted a very large amount of source-tree content: PR metadata reports 367 changed files and 1,018,461 deletions with no additions.
No PR body or human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The diff was not available in the slice because GitHub rejected it as exceeding the 300-file maximum for PR diffs.
decomp.dev's GALE01 report said "No changes", giving evidence that the removal was output-neutral for the tracked build.
smart_moves: Used automated GALE01 comparison as a safety check for a large deletion-only cleanup.
Kept the change mechanically simple: deletion-only, no mixed code edits visible in metadata.
Merged only after the bot reported no generated/binary changes, which is especially important when removing historical asm assets.
decomp_lessons: Large cleanup PRs can still be acceptable when they are mechanically scoped and backed by an output-equivalence report.
Once asm fallback material is no longer needed, removing it reduces repository weight and future search noise.
For massive deletions, rely on summary metadata and automated no-change reports when the normal GitHub diff is too large to inspect.
Future decomp cleanup work should avoid mixing behavior changes with tree-wide removals.
naming_conventions: The project used the top-level directory name asm/ for assembly material; this PR removes that convention rather than introducing a replacement.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No new matching tactic is visible in the slice; the notable tactic is validating a deletion-only removal of asm/ with a GALE01 no-change report.
The evidence suggests asm/ contents were no longer required for the build or comparison result covered by decomp.dev.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present.
The only recorded feedback was the decomp.dev bot report: "No changes" for GALE01.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that assume asm/ no longer exists.
Search for build scripts or documentation updated after PR 1623 to remove references to asm/.
Check whether any tooling still references asm/ after the mass deletion.
Identify the earlier PRs that fully eliminated the need for asm fallback files before this cleanup.
